reviews.author: Julia Kooper
reviews.text: Núi Đá Voi Mẹ, located in Yang Tao Commune of Lak District in Đắk Lắk Province, is one of the most extraordinary geological formations in Vietnam’s Central Highlands and is widely regarded as the largest monolithic granite rock in the country. Measuring approximately 200 meters in length, around 30 meters in height, and stretching nearly 500 meters in circumference at its base, this immense rock formation rises abruptly from the surrounding fields and forests, creating a dramatic and almost surreal landscape that immediately captures attention. Its name, meaning “Mother Elephant Rock Mountain,” derives from its remarkable resemblance to a giant elephant resting peacefully on the earth, a visual impression that becomes even more striking when viewed from a distance across the rural plains. The site is situated not far from Chu Yang Sin National Park and offers panoramic views over farmland, scattered ethnic villages, forested hills, and in the distance the peaks of the Chu Yang Sin mountain range, often referred to as the “roof” of the Central Highlands. The ascent to the top is relatively short, typically taking around 15–20 minutes, but the smooth granite surface requires careful footing, especially after rain when the rock can become slippery. From the summit, visitors are rewarded with sweeping, uninterrupted views that feel both vast and tranquil, making it an exceptional location for photography, sunset watching, and quiet contemplation. Beyond its geological scale, Núi Đá Voi Mẹ also carries cultural resonance for local communities, and nearby stands its counterpart, Đá Voi Cha (Father Elephant Rock), reinforcing the symbolic landscape that blends nature with folklore. The area remains relatively undeveloped, which preserves its raw and authentic atmosphere, allowing visitors to experience the untouched beauty of the highlands without excessive commercialization. For those seeking a distinctive natural landmark that combines geological grandeur, cultural symbolism, and breathtaking scenery, Núi Đá Voi Mẹ is an unforgettable destination in Dak Lak

reviews.author: Stanley Ler
reviews.text: A Hidden Gem at Elephant Rock 🐘🪨 ​If you’re looking for a scenic spot to unwind and catch some great views, Elephant Rock is definitely worth a visit. It offers a perfect blend of a little physical activity and a lot of relaxation. ​💰 Pricing & Value ​The entry fee is 60k per person, which is quite reasonable considering what’s included. One of the best perks is that the ticket comes with a free homemade drink—a refreshing touch that makes the climb even more rewarding. ​Family-Friendly Note: * Kids: My 5-year-old entered for free! ​Accessibility: While it involves a climb, it is surprisingly manageable for little ones. My 3-year-old was able to make it to the top. Of course, we held hands the entire way up for safety, but it was a proud moment for the kids ​🌟 The Experience ​Once you reach the top of the rock, the atmosphere is incredibly peaceful. It’s a nice place to chill out, feel the breeze, and take in the surrounding scenery. Whether you’re a photography enthusiast or just someone looking to escape the hustle and bustle, the "chill factor" here is 10/10. ​📍 Quick Tips for Visitors: ​Safety First: If you have toddlers, keep them close and hold hands during the ascent/descent as the surfaces can be uneven. ​Hydrate: Don't forget to claim your homemade drink! It’s the perfect way to cool down after the walk. ​Timing: Try to visit during the "golden hour" for the best photos and cooler temperatures. ​Overall Verdict: A budget-friendly, family-approved attraction that offers great views and a lovely vibe.

reviews.author: Sam Ng
reviews.text: The mother elephant rock is located inside a resort with 50k dong admission. Comes with a nice cooling chia seed drink. The view from the top was ok. Quite a number of visitors. Worth a visit if nearby.

reviews.author: Tuấn Vũ Trần
reviews.text: We came here in one of the hottest day in the past 30 years and it was also national holiday. But it was not bad since it was not so crowded and they have included 1 free drink per ticket, toilet was clean and the cafe area was well ventilated. The hard part is was climb this rock which is quite steep. My wife who always ask me to do more excecise must stop a few time to adjust her breathing and also her fear of height. I could climb it all at once but I got to stop and take the picture for her. Nice place to take picture and make fun of your wife though!

reviews.author: Matthew Withers
reviews.text: Fun drive through the country from BMT. Quaint, traditional houses upon arrival. Easy, yet challenging (height) climb to the top. It was a fun weekend activity. Enjoyed the drink too!

reviews.author: Ann Nguyen
reviews.text: It is a highly recommended place by locals. It was quite steep at the midpoint so I did not go up on the peak. People told me it was very chill up there cuz u get to see the mountain ranges from behind. No entrance fee for this place but we want to support locals giving them some tips. Locals are friendly, we should call them "đồng bào" don't say "dân tộc" they gonna be pissed. Worth coming for fun

reviews.author: Vishi Bansal
reviews.text: The elephant rock is fun only if you are there in the morning or evening, so that you can climb it. Otherwise just seeing it is OK.

reviews.author: Thuy Tien Phung
reviews.text: It was great in general but I would prefer Núi đá voi Cha. The place is not crowded, took 10.000 VND each go get it. Climbing up is easy but getting down is a challenge. Honestly I think it looks better from afar, not worth the climbing

reviews.author: Eric A.
reviews.text: We got to experience climbing a big rock hill that was the shape of a giant elephant. It was very windy for kids so we needed to be careful.

reviews.author: Đông Kỳ
reviews.text: Pretty good with the rice field in the sunny day. Not recommend for rainy days.

reviews.author: Nguyen Duc Nguyen
reviews.text: roughly translate to English is "Mother elephant rock mountain". Beautiful scene on the top. You can buy snack and drink here, they also have a restroom. No entrance and parking fee.

reviews.author: Nati
reviews.text: Beautiful natural rock! it's looks nice to climb it, but the behavior of the people there is a shame. They put a metal fence all over and forcing people to get from there entrance and pay 50k. Make business from selling food, drink or other stuff that you made by yourself, not from the nature itself. Two years ago the entrance cost only 10k, this people got really greedy

reviews.author: Xyz
reviews.text: 55k for entrance + one drink. The area is very beautiful and suitable for photos

reviews.author: Yehuda Halfon
reviews.text: Beautiful view once you make it to the tip of the rock.

reviews.author: Tâm Như Trần Nhật
reviews.text: worth visit, but it gonna be so sunny if u visit 10am-3pm

reviews.author: Son Nguyen
reviews.text: The unbelievable landscape in Daklak.

reviews.author: Boaz Dudai
reviews.text: A nice place to stop with a beautiful view and ponds of water lilies

reviews.author: Haim Kesselgross
reviews.text: Enough with all the artificial crap you force into every natural reservation.

reviews.author: Hưng
reviews.text: Climbing on top to see the whole view of krong bong , but be-careful of the wind

reviews.author: 난별이될거야
reviews.text: Amazing~~~~~ I strongly recommend to visit here~~~~

reviews.author: le thang
reviews.text: Very nice view point for the sunset

reviews.author: 武HEERA
reviews.text: It’s slippery- sneakers - sports shoes recommended

reviews.author: NOFOOD?
reviews.text: Woah. Very interesting. Looks hard to climb but it’s not. View is amazing!

reviews.author: Duong Hai Nguyen
reviews.text: Great place to take photos with your family and friends. 5/5

reviews.author: David Nguyen
reviews.text: The enormous rock looks really like a back of a elephant.
